mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment counts signals weakening aggregate demand and rising corporate default risk, which forces equity valuations lower and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized withdrawal of household balance sheet leverage, which contracts aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in industrial production below the 105 index level reflects severe supply-side bottlenecks and capacity utilization stress, which compresses corporate profit margins and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances despite the supply breakdown signal, or the forward return differential between triggered and non-triggered periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands corporate revenue expectations and improves earnings per share forecasts, which directly lifts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year Spearman correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of positive growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 215.0 trigger inflation hedging flows and anticipate tighter monetary policy, which raises real discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment rates confirm labor market normalization, which reduces wage-push inflation fears and supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized while consumer prices remain elevated creates a stagflationary environment that erodes corporate margins and equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of triggered instances despite the stagflation signal, or the cross-sectional predictive rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
